differential_diagnoses: Lymphoma (including primary mediastinal B-cell or extranodal NK/T-cell)
next_steps: Perform image-guided core needle biopsy of the retroperitoneal or mediastinal soft-tissue mass for histopathology and immunohistochemical staining (IgG4, CD20, CD3, etc.)
Initiate high-dose systemic glucocorticoids after biopsy to manage severe symptomatic effusions and inflammation
Perform PET-CT to evaluate for systemic involvement and identify optimal biopsy sites
Monitor QTc interval and cardiac rhythm given evidence of conduction abnormality
Consult cardiothoracic surgery for consideration of pericardial biopsy if non-invasive sampling is inconclusive

differential_diagnoses: Systemic vasculitis (e.g., Takayasu arteritis or large-vessel vasculitis)
Lymphoma (e.g., primary mediastinal B-cell or extranodal NK/T-cell)
next_steps: Perform ultrasound- or CT-guided core needle biopsy of the retroperitoneal or mediastinal mass for histopathology and immunohistochemical staining
Perform testing on biopsy specimen for BRAF V600E mutation to rule out Erdheim-Chester disease
Perform immunohistochemical staining on biopsy specimen for IgG4, CD20, CD3, and CD68 (histiocytic markers)
Order a skeletal survey or bone scintigraphy to look for pathognomonic osteosclerotic lesions associated with Erdheim-Chester disease
Perform PET-CT to evaluate systemic involvement and guide secondary biopsy sites if needed
Measure serum IgG4 levels and serum free light chains
Initiate cardiac monitoring and cardiology consultation for potential pericardial constriction
